channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: L’Oréal-UNESCO For Women in Science Sub-Saharan Africa Programme 2026: Fully Funded Research Grants for African Women Scientists
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The L’Oréal-UNESCO For Women in Science Sub-Saharan Africa Programme 2026 is now open for applications, offering an exceptional opportunity for women researchers across Africa to gain international recognition, funding, and professional development support. For over 25 years, the Fondation L’Oréal and UNESCO have collaborated to empower women in science globally. In 2026, this prestigious initiative […]

The post L’Oréal-UNESCO For Women in Science Sub-Saharan Africa Programme 2026: Fully Funded Research Grants for African Women Scientists appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The L’Oréal-UNESCO For Women in Science Sub-Saharan Africa Programme 2026 is now open for applications, offering an exceptional opportunity for women researchers across Africa to gain international recognition, funding, and professional development support.

For over 25 years, the Fondation L’Oréal and UNESCO have collaborated to empower women in science globally. In 2026, this prestigious initiative takes a bold step forward by merging the South African National Programme and the Sub-Saharan Africa Regional Programme into a single, unified Pan-African platform. This transformation creates a powerful, borderless network of scientific excellence across all 49 Sub-Saharan African countries.

This program is designed to identify, support, and elevate the next generation of African women scientists who are driving innovation and shaping the future of global research.

Program Overview

The 2026 edition of the programme seeks outstanding women researchers from leading laboratories and research institutions across Sub-Saharan Africa. It aims to strengthen scientific leadership on the continent while promoting gender equality in science.

Participants will become part of a highly respected international community of researchers and gain access to funding, training, and visibility on a global stage.

What the Programme Offers

Selected candidates will benefit from a combination of financial support and professional development opportunities, including:

1. Research Grants

  • 30 doctoral candidates will receive €10,000 each
  • 8 post-doctoral researchers will receive €15,000 each

These grants are designed to support research advancement, fieldwork, equipment, and academic development.

2. Capacity Building & Training

Participants will receive specialized training in:

  • Leadership development
  • Research management
  • Negotiation skills
  • Scientific communication

3. International Recognition

  • Join a prestigious network of award-winning scientists
  • Gain visibility within the global scientific community
  • Enhance academic and professional credibility

Eligible Research Fields

Applicants must be conducting research in one of the following disciplines:

  • Formal Sciences
  • Life Sciences
  • Environmental Sciences
  • Physical Sciences
  • Engineering
  • Technological Sciences

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for this programme, applicants must meet the following requirements:

Nationality & Location

  • Must be a citizen of a Sub-Saharan African country (including South Africa)
  • Must be conducting research within a recognized institution in Sub-Saharan Africa

For Doctoral Candidates

  • Must be currently enrolled in a PhD program
  • Must be in at least the second year of study (first-year students are not eligible)

For Post-Doctoral Researchers

  • Must already be engaged in postdoctoral research
  • Must have started research before the call opened
  • Must have defended their PhD thesis after February 2021
  • The eligibility window is extended by one year per child (maternity consideration)

General Condition

  • Previous recipients of any L’Oréal-UNESCO For Women in Science awards are not eligible

Selection Criteria

Applications will be evaluated based on:

  • Scientific excellence and innovation of the research project
  • Overall quality and clarity of the application
  • Ability to communicate scientific ideas effectively
  • Potential to contribute to scientific advancement and societal impact

Application Timeline

  • Call Opens: March 11, 2026
  • Application Deadline: May 15, 2026
  • Final Selection Announcement: July 6, 2026

Applicants are strongly encouraged to prepare and submit their applications well before the deadline.

Why You Should Apply

This programme is more than just a research grant—it is a gateway to global recognition and career advancement. By applying, you will:

  • Gain access to funding that supports impactful research
  • Strengthen your leadership and professional skills
  • Join a Pan-African network of leading women scientists
  • Position yourself as a change-maker in your field
  • Contribute to solving some of Africa’s most pressing challenges

How to Apply

To apply for the L’Oréal-UNESCO For Women in Science Sub-Saharan Africa Programme 2026:

  • Ensure you meet all eligibility requirements
  • Prepare a strong research proposal and supporting documents
  • Submit your application through the official platform

Click HERE to Apply

Learn more about the L’Oréal-UNESCO For Women in Science Sub-Saharan Africa Programme 2026 HERE.

Visit OFY HERE to discover more global Award opportunities, and click HERE for more global Grant opportunities.

The post L’Oréal-UNESCO For Women in Science Sub-Saharan Africa Programme 2026: Fully Funded Research Grants for African Women Scientists appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Climate Reality Leadership Training 2026 in Nashville, USA: Fully Funded Global Climate Leadership Opportunity
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
If you are passionate about climate action, sustainability, environmental advocacy, and global leadership, then the The Climate Reality Project Nashville Training 2026 is one of the most impactful international opportunities currently open for applications. This globally recognized program equips individuals with the knowledge, tools, and network needed to become influential climate leaders in their communities […]

The post Climate Reality Leadership Training 2026 in Nashville, USA: Fully Funded Global Climate Leadership Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are passionate about climate action, sustainability, environmental advocacy, and global leadership, then the The Climate Reality Project Nashville Training 2026 is one of the most impactful international opportunities currently open for applications. This globally recognized program equips individuals with the knowledge, tools, and network needed to become influential climate leaders in their communities and beyond.

What Is the Climate Reality Leadership Corps Training?

The Climate Reality Leadership Corps training is a high-impact global initiative designed to train individuals to become effective climate advocates and communicators.

Participants will:

  • Train directly with former U.S. Vice President Al Gore and leading climate experts
  • Gain a deep understanding of the climate crisis and practical solutions
  • Learn advocacy, communication, and organizing skills
  • Join a global network of over tens of thousands of climate leaders across more than 190 countries

The program has trained thousands of individuals worldwide to lead climate action efforts at local, national, and global levels.

Why You Should Apply: Key Benefits of the Program

1. World-Class Training and Mentorship – Participants receive direct training from globally recognized experts, including Al Gore, who has been a leading voice in climate advocacy for decades.

2. Global Networking Opportunities – You will join a diverse network of climate leaders, activists, policymakers, and professionals from across continents, enabling long-term collaborations.

3. Certification as a Climate Reality Leader – Upon completion, you become an official Climate Reality Leader, opening doors to global advocacy platforms and initiatives.

4. Access to Tools and Resources – Participants gain access to:

  • Advocacy toolkits
  • Communication materials
  • Ongoing training and mentorship programs
  • Global campaigns and community platforms

5. Career and Impact Growth – This opportunity is ideal for building a career in:

  • Climate policy
  • Environmental advocacy
  • Sustainable development
  • International development

Who Can Apply? (Eligibility Criteria)

The program is highly inclusive and designed for global participation.

Eligible Applicants Include:

  • Students (high school, undergraduate, postgraduate)
  • Young professionals
  • Activists and community leaders
  • Entrepreneurs and innovators
  • Policy advocates
  • Educators and researchers

Age Requirements:

  •  Minimum age:
    • 13 years (U.S. applicants)
    • 16 years (international applicants)

Nationality Requirements:

  • Open to applicants worldwide
  • Individuals from Africa, Asia, Europe, North America, South America, and Oceania are eligible

The program is intentionally global, encouraging diverse perspectives in climate action.

Required Qualifications

Unlike many competitive international programs, this training does not require strict academic qualifications. However, strong applicants typically demonstrate:

  • Passion for climate action and sustainability
  • Interest in advocacy, leadership, or community engagement
  • Commitment to implementing climate solutions locally
  • Ability to communicate and influence change

No specific degree, GPA, or professional experience is required—making it accessible to a wide range of applicants.

What Does the Training Cover?

The Nashville training is an intensive, immersive experience focused on real-world climate leadership.

Key Learning Areas:

  • Climate science and global impacts
  • Renewable energy and clean solutions
  • Climate justice and equity
  • Public speaking and communication strategies
  • Grassroots organizing and mobilization
  • Policy advocacy and political engagement

Participants will also learn how to:

  • Deliver impactful climate presentations
  • Organize campaigns
  • Influence decision-makers
  • Build community-based climate initiatives

Financial Information: Is It Fully Funded?

The program is free to attend, but it is not fully funded.

What Is Covered:

  • Training participation
  • Some meals during the event

What You Must Cover:

  • International or local travel
  • Accommodation
  • Additional meals

However, scholarship opportunities may be available, and selected applicants may receive financial assistance depending on availability.

Application Process

To apply, candidates must:

  1. Complete the online application form via the official website
  2. Provide personal and professional details
  3. Demonstrate motivation and interest in climate leadership
  4. Submit before the deadline

Selection Process:

  • Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis
  • Selected candidates receive an email confirmation
  • Final participation requires confirmation and logistics planning

Apply here

Why This Opportunity Matters in 2026

The 2026 Nashville training is particularly significant because:

  • It marks 20 years of climate leadership training by the organization
  • It brings together global leaders at a critical time for climate action
  • It aims to expand the global network of climate advocates

With climate change becoming one of the most urgent global challenges, this training empowers individuals to drive real change.

Deadline for Application

  • Deadline: Not strictly fixed (Rolling applications)
  • Recommendation: Apply as early as possible before slots are filled

Since applications are reviewed continuously, early applicants have a higher chance of selection.

The The Climate Reality Project Nashville Training 2026 is not just a conference—it is a gateway to becoming a global climate leader, joining a powerful international network, and gaining the skills needed to influence real-world change.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Climate Reality Leadership Training 2026 in Nashville, USA: Fully Funded Global Climate Leadership Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Youth Advocacy Coalition Internship 2026: A Fully Funded Mental Health Advocacy Opportunity for Young Leaders Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
The Youth Advocacy Coalition (YAC) Internship Program by The Jed Foundation is one of the most impactful global opportunities for young people passionate about mental health, policy advocacy, and youth leadership. Designed to empower the next generation of changemakers, this program equips participants with practical advocacy skills, real-world policy exposure, and a platform to influence […]

The post Youth Advocacy Coalition Internship 2026: A Fully Funded Mental Health Advocacy Opportunity for Young Leaders Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Youth Advocacy Coalition (YAC) Internship Program by The Jed Foundation is one of the most impactful global opportunities for young people passionate about mental health, policy advocacy, and youth leadership. Designed to empower the next generation of changemakers, this program equips participants with practical advocacy skills, real-world policy exposure, and a platform to influence decision-makers.

If you are a student or young leader looking to make a difference in mental health systems, this internship could be your gateway into national and global advocacy.

About the Youth Advocacy Coalition (YAC)

The Youth Advocacy Coalition was created to amplify youth voices in mental health policy and reform. It brings together passionate young advocates and connects them with policymakers, organizations, and institutions working to improve mental health systems.

The initiative was launched in collaboration with advocacy partners to ensure that young people play a central role in shaping policies that directly affect their lives.

Participants are selected based on their impact in their communities and commitment to mental health advocacy, making this a highly competitive and prestigious opportunity.

What the Internship Offers

1. Paid Internship Experience

The YAC internship is a paid opportunity, ensuring that participants are financially supported while contributing to meaningful advocacy work.

2. High-Level Advocacy Training

Interns undergo structured training in:

  • Mental health policy and systems
  • Advocacy strategies
  • Public storytelling and media engagement
  • Civic participation and leadership

3. Mentorship from Experts

Participants receive guidance from:

  • Policy experts
  • Mental health professionals
  • Advocacy leaders

4. Real Policy Engagement

Interns engage directly with:

  • Government officials
  • Advocacy organizations
  • Policy stakeholders

They also participate in Advocacy Days on Capitol Hill, gaining firsthand experience in influencing public policy.

5. National Exposure

Interns present their work at major events such as:

  • JED’s Annual Policy Summit
  • National advocacy forums

This exposure helps build credibility and opens doors to future opportunities.

Program Structure and Activities

The internship follows a hands-on, project-based learning model, including:

  • Weekly training sessions on advocacy and policy
  • Development of a strategic mental health advocacy project
  • Collaboration with fellow youth leaders
  • Engagement in awareness campaigns and storytelling

Participants also continue their advocacy work beyond the internship, extending impact throughout the academic year.

Who Can Apply (Eligibility Criteria)

Target Category of Applicants

This opportunity is designed for:

  • Undergraduate students
  • Young leaders and youth advocates
  • Individuals passionate about mental health and policy change

Key Qualifications

Applicants are typically expected to:

  • Demonstrate strong interest or experience in mental health advocacy
  • Show leadership and community impact
  • Have the ability to collaborate and communicate effectively
  • Be committed to driving social change

Participants in the broader coalition are often selected through a rigorous vetting process based on their advocacy impact and leadership.

Eligible Countries and Regions

  • Primarily open to students in the United States (core program focus)
  • However, international students studying in U.S. institutions may also be eligible
  • Some virtual components and advocacy engagement may allow global participation depending on cohort structure

Because program specifics may evolve annually, applicants from:

  • Africa
  • Asia
  • Europe
  • Latin America

are encouraged to monitor updates, especially if enrolled in international or U.S.-affiliated institutions.

Why You Should Apply

1. Build a Career in Policy and Advocacy – This internship provides a direct pathway into policy-making and public health advocacy careers.

2. Gain Real-World Impact – Participants contribute to actual policy discussions and reforms, not just theoretical learning.

3. Join a Powerful Network – You will connect with:

  • Youth leaders
  • Policymakers
  • Advocacy organizations

4. Address a Global Crisis – With rising rates of depression and anxiety among young people, initiatives like YAC are critical in shaping mental health solutions.

Application Process (General Guide)

While exact steps may vary yearly, applicants typically:

  1. Complete an online application form
  2. Submit personal and academic details
  3. Provide evidence of advocacy or leadership experience
  4. Possibly attend interviews or screening sessions

Selection is highly competitive, so demonstrating real impact and passion is essential.

Apply here

Deadline

10th April 2026

The Youth Advocacy Coalition Internship by is more than just an internship—it is a transformational leadership experience. It empowers young people to step into decision-making spaces and actively shape the future of mental health policy.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Youth Advocacy Coalition Internship 2026: A Fully Funded Mental Health Advocacy Opportunity for Young Leaders Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded Humanitarian Scholarship in Irish Studies at the University of Liverpool (2026)
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
The University of Liverpool is offering a life-changing opportunity for postgraduate students through its Humanitarian Scholarship in Irish Studies. This prestigious initiative, delivered in partnership with the Institute of Irish Studies, is designed to support a Palestinian student in pursuing an MRes degree with full financial backing. This scholarship is more than just funding—it’s a […]

The post Fully Funded Humanitarian Scholarship in Irish Studies at the University of Liverpool (2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Liverpool is offering a life-changing opportunity for postgraduate students through its Humanitarian Scholarship in Irish Studies. This prestigious initiative, delivered in partnership with the Institute of Irish Studies, is designed to support a Palestinian student in pursuing an MRes degree with full financial backing.

This scholarship is more than just funding—it’s a pathway to academic growth, global exposure, and meaningful research connecting Ireland and Palestine.

🎓 About the Scholarship

The Humanitarian Scholarship in Irish Studies aims to empower students who have faced displacement or barriers to education by offering them the opportunity to study in the UK.

The MRes program focuses on interdisciplinary research, allowing scholars to explore topics such as:

  • Irish history (medieval and modern)
  • Politics and peace/conflict studies
  • Literature and language
  • Connections between Ireland and Palestine

Students will complete a major research project aligned with faculty expertise, enabling them to contribute valuable insights to global academic discussions.

💰 Scholarship Benefits

This is a fully funded scholarship that provides comprehensive support, including:

  • Full tuition fee waiver
  • Accommodation coverage
  • Living stipend of up to £18,688
  • Travel costs to the UK (arranged by the university)
  • Meet-and-greet support upon arrival
  • Visa and language support services
  • Additional benefits such as discounted gym membership

This package ensures that scholars can focus entirely on their studies without financial stress.

✅ Eligibility Criteria

To qualify, applicants must:

  • Apply for the Irish Studies MRes program at the University of Liverpool
  • Be a Palestinian student registered with UNRWA or from Gaza/West Bank (with proof)
  • Be currently residing outside the UK

📝 Application Process

Step 1: Apply for the MRes Program

Submit your application for the Irish Studies MRes via the university’s official course page.

  • Include a strong personal statement
  • Highlight your interest in Ireland–Palestine connections

Deadline for course application: 1 April 2026

Step 2: Apply for the Scholarship

Once you’ve applied for the course:

  • Complete the scholarship application form
  • Submit proof of UNRWA registration or residency in Gaza/West Bank

Deadline for scholarship application: 1 May 2026

Step 3: Admission Offer

Applicants may receive:

  • Conditional offer (requires additional documents or English test)
  • Unconditional offer

Step 4: Meet Offer Conditions

If required:

  • Submit academic documents
  • Provide English proficiency (Duolingo accepted)

📅 Important Dates

  • Course Application Deadline: 1 April 2026
  • Scholarship Deadline: 1 May 2026
  • Result Notification: Early June 2026

📢 Final Notes

  • Applicants must secure a UK Student Visa (costs reimbursed after arrival)
  • Initial personal funds are needed for early expenses in the UK
  • Accommodation is arranged by the university

This scholarship represents a powerful opportunity for students seeking to rebuild their academic journey while contributing to meaningful global research.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more scholarships for master opportunities, click here. 

The post Fully Funded Humanitarian Scholarship in Irish Studies at the University of Liverpool (2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded UCL & V&A PhD Studentship 2026: Study Migrant Labour and British Craft in the 18th Century
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
Are you passionate about history, museums, and uncovering hidden narratives? A prestigious fully funded PhD studentship is now open for applications through University College London (UCL) and the Victoria and Albert Museum (V&A) under the AHRC Collaborative Doctoral Partnership (CDP) scheme. This unique opportunity allows doctoral researchers to explore the untold contributions of migrant labour […]

The post Fully Funded UCL & V&A PhD Studentship 2026: Study Migrant Labour and British Craft in the 18th Century appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you passionate about history, museums, and uncovering hidden narratives? A prestigious fully funded PhD studentship is now open for applications through University College London (UCL) and the Victoria and Albert Museum (V&A) under the AHRC Collaborative Doctoral Partnership (CDP) scheme.

This unique opportunity allows doctoral researchers to explore the untold contributions of migrant labour to British craft and design during the long eighteenth century, while gaining hands-on experience with one of the world’s leading museums.

📅 Key Details

  • Start Date: 1 October 2026
  • Application Deadline: 15 April 2026 (23:59 UK time)
  • Interview Date: 15 May 2026 (online)
  • Location: London (UCL + V&A, hybrid engagement)
  • Duration: 4 years full-time (or part-time equivalent)

🎓 About the PhD Project

The project, titled “Invisible Hands: Migrant Labour and British Craft in the Long Eighteenth Century,” investigates how migrant artisans shaped Britain’s material culture between 1688 and 1837.

Despite the recognition of some groups like Huguenot silk weavers, many migrant contributors remain hidden in archives. This research aims to uncover those overlooked stories using a combination of:

  • Historical research
  • Museum and archival analysis
  • Digital humanities techniques

You will work with extensive collections including furniture, textiles, ceramics, and metalwork, while exploring archival materials such as apprenticeship records, guild registers, and court documents.

🔍 Research Focus Areas

Possible research questions include:

  • How can migrant craftspeople be identified in historical archives?
  • What patterns of labour, mobility, and collaboration existed?
  • How visible are migrant contributions in museum collections?
  • How can digital tools help uncover hidden histories?

The project is flexible, allowing you to tailor your research focus based on your academic interests.

🏛️ What Makes This Opportunity Unique?

This is a collaborative PhD between a top global university and a world-renowned museum. As a student, you will:

  • Work closely with expert supervisors from both institutions
  • Gain hands-on experience with museum collections and archives
  • Develop skills in cataloguing, curatorial practice, and public engagement
  • Join a national network of CDP-funded researchers

💰 Funding and Benefits

This fully funded studentship includes:

  • Full tuition fee coverage (including international fee waiver)
  • Annual stipend of £21,805 + £2,000 London weighting
  • Additional £600 yearly maintenance payment
  • Up to £1,250 for travel and research expenses
  • Access to professional development and networking opportunities

📚 Eligibility Criteria

Applicants should:

  • Hold (or expect) a Master’s degree in a relevant field such as History, Art History, Museum Studies, Digital Humanities, Anthropology, or related disciplines
  • Demonstrate interest in the museum and heritage sector
  • Show potential for research and interdisciplinary collaboration

Both home and international applicants are eligible, with international students required to reside in the UK during the program.

📝 Application Process

To apply:

  1. Submit your PhD application through the UCL online admissions system
  2. Include the project title and supervisor names
  3. Prepare the following documents:
    • CV (max 2 pages)
    • Research statement (max 1 page)
    • Cover letter (max 2 pages)
    • Optional writing sample

There is no application fee.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more PhD opportunities, click here. 

📢 Why You Should Apply

This PhD is more than just a degree—it’s an opportunity to:

  • Contribute to rewriting history by uncovering marginalized voices
  • Gain real-world museum experience
  • Build a strong academic and professional network
  • Develop cutting-edge digital and archival research skills

If you are driven by curiosity, historical inquiry, and cultural research, this studentship offers an exceptional pathway to advance your academic career.

The post Fully Funded UCL & V&A PhD Studentship 2026: Study Migrant Labour and British Craft in the 18th Century appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: APNIC Foundation 2026 Research Fellowship: $10,000 Grant for Digital Innovation in Asia Pacific
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The APNIC Foundation has officially opened applications for its 2026 Research Fellowship, a prestigious program designed to support high-impact, evidence-based research addressing critical digital challenges across the Asia Pacific region. Following the success of its inaugural cohort, the 2026 edition expands its commitment to empowering researchers, technical experts, and policy professionals to shape the future […]

The post APNIC Foundation 2026 Research Fellowship: $10,000 Grant for Digital Innovation in Asia Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The APNIC Foundation has officially opened applications for its 2026 Research Fellowship, a prestigious program designed to support high-impact, evidence-based research addressing critical digital challenges across the Asia Pacific region.

Following the success of its inaugural cohort, the 2026 edition expands its commitment to empowering researchers, technical experts, and policy professionals to shape the future of digital infrastructure, artificial intelligence, and inclusive technology systems.

This fellowship is not just about research—it is about influencing real-world policy, strengthening digital resilience, and ensuring that technological advancement benefits all communities across the region.

🌏 Why This Fellowship Matters in 2026

The Asia Pacific region is undergoing rapid digital transformation. However, this progress is uneven. While some economies are advancing in artificial intelligence and digital systems, many communities still lack reliable Internet access and inclusive infrastructure.

This imbalance creates serious risks, including:

  • Widening socio-economic inequality
  • Fragile and vulnerable Internet infrastructure
  • Ethical and sustainability concerns around AI
  • Fragmented digital systems lacking interoperability

The APNIC Foundation Research Fellowship directly addresses these challenges by supporting locally grounded, policy-relevant research that bridges the gap between technical expertise and real-world implementation.

💡 Fellowship Overview

The 2026 APNIC Foundation Research Fellowship offers:

  • 💰 USD 10,000 stipend for each selected fellow
  • 👩‍🔬 5 fellowship positions available
  • 🗓️ Duration: 8 months (April – November 2026)
  • 🌍 Format: Fully remote (global collaboration, local impact)

Fellows will conduct independent research projects that contribute to strengthening digital ecosystems and informing policy decisions across the Asia Pacific.

🔍 Research Focus Areas (Thematic Tracks)

Applicants must align their proposals with one of the following three tracks:

1. Internet Infrastructure and Resilience

This track focuses on the backbone of digital connectivity, addressing vulnerabilities in network systems and ensuring long-term stability.

Key topics include:

  • Internet routing security
  • Regional traffic measurement
  • Subsea cable resilience
  • Network reliability under geopolitical pressure

2. Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI)

This track explores systems that enable essential services such as digital identity, payments, and data exchange.

Key topics include:

  • Interoperability of digital systems
  • Cross-border infrastructure
  • Financial inclusion and last-mile connectivity
  • Bridging the digital divide

3. Sustainable AI

This track tackles the environmental and operational challenges of artificial intelligence systems.

Key topics include:

  • Energy efficiency of AI systems
  • Environmental impact of data centres
  • Sustainable compute infrastructure
  • AI solutions for environmental challenges

🧠 What Fellows Will Do

Selected fellows will:

  • Conduct original, high-quality research
  • Develop policy-relevant insights and recommendations
  • Produce outputs such as:
    • Research papers
    • Measurement tools
    • Proofs of concept
    • Strategic frameworks

The APNIC Foundation may publish or integrate these outputs into broader regional initiatives, amplifying their impact.

👩‍💼 Who Should Apply?

The fellowship is open to experienced professionals across three key profiles:

Researchers

  • Experts in data science, economics, or social sciences
  • Skilled in quantitative modeling and impact analysis
  • Able to translate complex data into policy insights

Technical Practitioners

  • Engineers, system architects, infrastructure specialists
  • Experience in real-world digital system deployment
  • Ability to develop technical frameworks and solutions

Policy Professionals

  • Consultants, legal experts, government advisors
  • Skilled in policy drafting and strategic planning
  • Ability to connect technical research with national development goals

✅ Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must:

  • Reside in one of the 56 Asia Pacific economies served by the APNIC Foundation
  • Have 7–10 years of professional experience
  • Hold at least a Master’s degree in a relevant field
  • Have at least one published research paper (first author)
  • Be willing to publish outputs under a Creative Commons (CC BY 4.0) license

📅 Key Dates

  • Applications Open: 10 March 2026
  • Application Deadline: 31 March 2026 (11:59 PM UTC+10)
  • Selection Announcement: 15 April 2026
  • Fellowship Period: 15 April – 30 November 2026

🌐 How to Apply

Interested applicants should visit the official APNIC Foundation Fellowships page to:

  • Access detailed guidelines
  • Review eligibility requirements
  • Submit their application

Make sure your proposal is aligned with one of the thematic tracks and clearly demonstrates both technical depth and policy relevance.

For more information click here 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post APNIC Foundation 2026 Research Fellowship: $10,000 Grant for Digital Innovation in Asia Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Emerging Leaders’ Programme 2026 in Brussels: Fully Funded EU Leadership Opportunity for Young Professionals in the UK
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The Emerging Leaders’ Programme 2026, organized by the Delegation of the European Union to the United Kingdom in cooperation with the European Parliament, is now open for applications. This prestigious leadership initiative is designed to equip early-career professionals in the UK with in-depth knowledge of European Union institutions, policies, and international relations. This fully funded […]

The post Emerging Leaders’ Programme 2026 in Brussels: Fully Funded EU Leadership Opportunity for Young Professionals in the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Emerging Leaders’ Programme 2026, organized by the Delegation of the European Union to the United Kingdom in cooperation with the European Parliament, is now open for applications. This prestigious leadership initiative is designed to equip early-career professionals in the UK with in-depth knowledge of European Union institutions, policies, and international relations.

This fully funded programme offers a unique opportunity to participate in a five-day immersive study visit to Brussels, where selected participants will engage directly with EU officials, policymakers, and fellow emerging leaders from across sectors.

The programme aims to strengthen EU–UK relations by fostering mutual understanding, collaboration, and dialogue among future leaders.

About the Emerging Leaders’ Programme 2026

The Emerging Leaders’ Programme is a targeted initiative focused on:

  • Introducing participants to the structure and functioning of EU institutions
  • Enhancing understanding of EU policies across various sectors
  • Strengthening EU–UK relations through engagement and collaboration
  • Building a network of future leaders across government, politics, and civil society

Participants will gain first-hand exposure to how the European Union operates and its role in global affairs.

Programme Duration and Location

  • Programme Dates: 22 June – 26 June 2026
  • Location: Brussels, Belgium
  • Format: In-person study visit

The programme spans five days and includes a comprehensive schedule of meetings, discussions, and networking activities.

Programme Objectives

The Emerging Leaders’ Programme is designed to:

  1. Familiarize participants with EU institutions and governance systems
  2. Provide insights into EU policymaking and international relations
  3. Facilitate cross-sector collaboration between UK professionals and EU stakeholders
  4. Strengthen dialogue and cooperation between the United Kingdom and the European Union

What the Programme Includes

Participants selected for the programme will receive:

  • Fully funded travel (flights or trains to Brussels)
  • Local transportation within Brussels
  • Accommodation for the duration of the programme
  • Meals (breakfast, lunch, and dinner arrangements)
  • Access to a structured programme itinerary
  • Pre-programme briefing sessions and materials
  • Engagement with EU officials and thought leaders

Key Benefits of the Programme

Participants will benefit from:

  • Direct exposure to EU institutions and decision-making processes
  • Networking opportunities with emerging leaders and policymakers
  • Participation in roundtable discussions on EU–UK collaboration
  • Enhanced understanding of international diplomacy and governance
  • Opportunities to contribute to strengthening EU–UK relations

Programme Activities and Content

The programme offers a dynamic and interactive learning experience, including:

  • Meetings with EU officials and institutional representatives
  • Roundtable discussions on policy and cooperation
  • Networking sessions with professionals from diverse sectors
  • Briefings on EU institutions and EU–UK relations
  • Collaborative exchanges and knowledge-sharing sessions

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Be based in the United Kingdom
  • Be aged between 20 and 35 years (flexibility may apply)
  • Be a working professional in one of the following sectors:
    • Government
    • Political organizations
    • Civil society
  • Demonstrate:
    • Leadership or emerging leadership potential
    • Engagement in public discourse or community initiatives
    • Interest in EU–UK relations

Applicants should also be able to commit to the full programme, including pre- and post-programme activities.

Why You Should Apply

The Emerging Leaders’ Programme 2026 is an exceptional opportunity for young professionals to:

  • Gain practical insights into EU governance and policymaking
  • Build valuable international networks
  • Enhance leadership and diplomatic skills
  • Contribute to shaping the future of EU–UK relations
  • Advance their careers in public policy, governance, and international relations

This programme is ideal for individuals seeking to make meaningful contributions to public discourse and international cooperation.

Application Requirements

To apply, candidates must submit:

  1. Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  2. Completed application form

Applications must be sent via email with the subject line:
“ELP 2026 Application”

Application Deadline

  • Deadline: 12 April 2026 (23:59 BST)

Late applications will not be considered.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should download and fill this form, Emerging Leaders Programme – Application form 2026.docx and submit it together with the required application documents via email: DELEGATION-UNITED-KINGDOM-POLITICAL@eeas.europa.eu

For full details, click HERE.

Visit OFY HERE for more global fellowship opportunities.

The post Emerging Leaders’ Programme 2026 in Brussels: Fully Funded EU Leadership Opportunity for Young Professionals in the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Junior Economist and Modeler at the OECD (Paris, France): Launch Your Career in Global Economic Policy
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) is offering an exceptional opportunity for early-career economists to join its prestigious Economics Department as a Junior Economist and Modeler in Paris, France. This full-time position places you at the heart of global economic policymaking, working alongside leading experts to shape policies that impact economies worldwide. If […]

The post Junior Economist and Modeler at the OECD (Paris, France): Launch Your Career in Global Economic Policy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) is offering an exceptional opportunity for early-career economists to join its prestigious Economics Department as a Junior Economist and Modeler in Paris, France. This full-time position places you at the heart of global economic policymaking, working alongside leading experts to shape policies that impact economies worldwide.

If you are passionate about economic research, data-driven policy analysis, and international collaboration, this role offers a unique platform to grow your career in one of the most influential international organizations.

About the OECD and Its Mission

The OECD is an international organization composed of 38 member countries, dedicated to building better policies for better lives. It works closely with governments, policymakers, and stakeholders to promote economic growth, social inclusion, and environmental sustainability.

Through data analysis, research, and international collaboration, the OECD addresses some of the world’s most pressing challenges, including job creation, education reform, economic resilience, and global tax systems.

About the Economics Department (ECO)

The Economics Department plays a central role in shaping OECD’s policy advice. It focuses on:

  • Macroeconomic analysis
  • Structural policy development
  • Country-specific and cross-country research
  • Evidence-based policymaking

Its work contributes directly to major OECD publications such as Economic Outlook, Economic Surveys, and policy reports that influence decision-makers worldwide.

Role Overview: Junior Economist and Modeler

As a Junior Economist and Modeler, you will join the Structural Policy and Research Division (ECO/SPRD). You will contribute to cutting-edge research on macroeconomic and structural models, helping generate insights that guide economic policies across countries.

You will work under the supervision of a Senior Economist and collaborate with a wide range of stakeholders, including:

  • Governments and central banks
  • International organizations
  • Research institutions and think tanks
  • Academic experts

Key Responsibilities

1. Modelling and Policy Analysis

  • Contribute to the development of macroeconomic and structural models
  • Conduct quantitative analyses tailored to specific countries
  • Support policy recommendations based on empirical research
  • Assist in coordinating modelling work within the division
  • Provide technical guidance to research assistants and trainees

2. Research and Drafting

  • Draft analytical papers and reports for OECD committees and publications
  • Summarize findings from economic models and research
  • Contribute to flagship OECD outputs such as:
    • Economic Surveys
    • Working Papers
    • Economic Outlook
    • Policy Papers

3. Data Collection and Management

  • Gather and compile economic data
  • Ensure data quality, consistency, and comparability
  • Build and manage databases for research purposes

4. Liaison and Outreach

  • Build professional networks with policymakers and researchers
  • Engage with international stakeholders
  • Support workshops and collaborative initiatives

5. Additional Contributions

  • Assist in organizing events and workshops
  • Proofread and review analytical documents
  • Perform other related tasks as required

Ideal Candidate Profile

Academic Requirements

  • Advanced university degree in Economics
  • A PhD is considered an advantage

Professional Experience

  • 1–2 years of experience in:
    • Economic statistics
    • Econometrics (time-series and panel data)
    • Applied economic modeling
  • Experience in research, internships, or analytical roles is highly valued

Technical Skills

  • Strong knowledge of econometric techniques
  • Ability to manage large datasets
  • Programming skills in:
    • Python (essential)
    • MATLAB (essential)
    • R and Stata (advantageous)

Core Competencies

  • Strong communication and teamwork skills
  • Analytical thinking and problem-solving abilities
  • Capacity to innovate and adapt in a dynamic environment

Language Requirements

  • Fluency in English or French
  • Working knowledge of the other language (or willingness to learn)
  • Additional languages are a plus

Salary and Benefits

  • Starting monthly salary: 6,008.38 EUR (tax-exempt in France)
  • Additional allowances depending on eligibility
  • Opportunity to work in a highly international environment
  • Access to global research networks and high-level policy discussions
  • Possibility of missions and international exposure

Contract Details

  • Multiple positions available
  • Contract type determined based on organizational needs
  • Possibility of appointment at a lower grade depending on experience

Selection Process

Shortlisted candidates may go through:

  • Job-based assessments
  • Panel interviews

The OECD promotes diversity and equal opportunity and encourages applications from all qualified candidates from member countries.

📅 Application Deadline

Applications must be submitted no later than 7 April 2026 (midnight CEST). Early applications are strongly encouraged as the position may be filled before the deadline.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should apply through the official OECD careers portal. Make sure your application includes:

  • Updated CV
  • Motivation letter
  • Relevant academic and professional references

For more information click here 

Discover more opportunities here 

 

The post Junior Economist and Modeler at the OECD (Paris, France): Launch Your Career in Global Economic Policy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Mastercard Foundation Associates Program 2026: 12-Month Paid Internship Opportunity for Young African Graduates Across 22+ Countries
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The Mastercard Foundation Associates Program 2026 is a transformative career development initiative designed to support young African graduates in their transition from education into meaningful employment, entrepreneurship, and leadership roles. This highly impactful program offers 12-month paid internship placements across Africa, connecting talented youth with organizations that are shaping the future of the continent. Launched […]

The post Mastercard Foundation Associates Program 2026: 12-Month Paid Internship Opportunity for Young African Graduates Across 22+ Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Mastercard Foundation Associates Program 2026 is a transformative career development initiative designed to support young African graduates in their transition from education into meaningful employment, entrepreneurship, and leadership roles. This highly impactful program offers 12-month paid internship placements across Africa, connecting talented youth with organizations that are shaping the future of the continent.

Launched to address the growing need for structured career pathways for young people, the program equips participants with hands-on work experience, leadership development, mentorship, and professional networking opportunities. Since its inception in 2023, the program has successfully supported over 1,000 young professionals across 22 African countries, including young women, persons with disabilities, refugees, and internally displaced persons.

The Mastercard Foundation Associates Program continues to expand its reach, offering ongoing application opportunities for eligible candidates across different regions in Africa.

About the Mastercard Foundation Associates Program

The Mastercard Foundation Associates Program is a flagship initiative under the Mastercard Foundation’s broader mission to enable young people in Africa to access dignified and fulfilling work opportunities.

The program is built on strategic partnerships with:

  • Leading employers

  • Development organizations

  • Recruitment platforms

  • Foundation partner institutions

Through these partnerships, the program provides structured internship placements that help young professionals gain relevant work experience and develop critical career skills.

The Associates Program focuses on:

  • Bridging the gap between education and employment

  • Supporting youth employability and entrepreneurship

  • Building leadership capacity among young Africans

  • Promoting inclusive participation across diverse groups

Program Focus Areas

The Mastercard Foundation Associates Program prioritizes inclusivity and impact across several thematic areas.

1. Disability Inclusion

The program ensures equal opportunities for young people with disabilities, providing accessible pathways into employment and professional development.

Read More

2. Education and Career Transitions

The initiative supports graduates in transitioning from academic life into professional careers or entrepreneurship, addressing the gap between education systems and labor market demands.

Read More

3. Refugees and Displaced Persons

The program actively includes refugees and internally displaced youth, offering them opportunities to rebuild their careers and livelihoods through structured internships.

Read More

Program Structure and Journey

The Associates Program follows a structured journey that supports participants from application to career transition.

1. Recruitment and Selection

Applicants are selected based on eligibility criteria, skills, and potential to benefit from the program.

2. Internship Placement

Successful candidates are placed in 12-month internship roles with partner organizations across Africa or in remote positions.

3. Transition Pathways

After completing the internship, participants receive support to transition into:

  • Full-time employment

  • Entrepreneurship opportunities

  • Advanced career pathways

What Participants Gain

The Mastercard Foundation Associates Program offers a comprehensive package of benefits designed to prepare participants for long-term career success.

1. 12-Month Internship Placement

Participants are placed in structured internship roles that provide real-world professional experience.

2. Hands-On Work Experience

Associates gain practical exposure by working on real projects within organizations, enhancing their technical and professional competencies.

3. Leadership, Employability, and Entrepreneurship Skills

The program includes training and development opportunities that focus on:

  • Leadership development

  • Workplace readiness

  • Entrepreneurial thinking

  • Problem-solving and innovation

4. Mentorship and Professional Networks

Participants receive guidance from experienced professionals and gain access to valuable networks that support career growth.

5. Monthly Stipend

Associates receive a stipend to support them financially throughout the duration of the internship.

6. Career Transition Support

The program provides structured support to help participants move into employment or entrepreneurship after completing their internships.

Why You Should Apply

The Mastercard Foundation Associates Program is one of the most impactful early-career opportunities available to young Africans today.

It offers:

  • A direct pathway from education to employment

  • Exposure to professional work environments

  • Access to global networks and mentors

  • Practical skills for long-term career success

  • Opportunities to contribute to meaningful work across Africa

For young graduates seeking to build strong careers, this program provides a powerful foundation for professional growth and impact.

Who Can Apply

The program is open to a wide range of young people across Africa who are seeking early-career opportunities.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  1. Be aged between 18 and 35 years

  2. Be a recent graduate with 0–2 years of work experience

  3. Be a Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program Alumni or alumni of Mastercard Foundation programs (preferred but not always mandatory depending on roles)

  4. Be a young person residing in Africa

  5. Be seeking meaningful early-career experience

Commitment to Inclusion

The Mastercard Foundation Associates Program is intentionally inclusive and aims to ensure representation from:

  • Young women

  • Persons with disabilities

  • Refugees and internally displaced persons

  • Youth from underserved and marginalized communities

This inclusive approach ensures that the program contributes to equitable access to opportunities across Africa.

Where and How to Apply

Applicants must apply based on their country of residence or eligible remote roles. Applications are open on a rolling basis, depending on available opportunities in each region.

Applicants can access tips and guidance joining the Mastercard Foundation Transitions Community on LinkedIn.

Application Platforms by Region

1. South Africa

  • Apply through the Youth Employment Service (YES)

2. Anglophone West Africa

  • Apply through Jobberman

3. Francophone Africa

  • Apply through Talent2Africa

4. East Africa

  • Apply through Shortlist Futures

5. Remote Opportunities

  • Apply through Talentum

6. Southern Africa

  • Applications expected to open soon

Applicants are advised to monitor these platforms regularly, as roles are posted continuously.

Additional Resources for Applicants

Interested candidates can join the Mastercard Foundation Transitions Community on LinkedIn, where they can access:

  • Application tips

  • Career guidance

  • Program updates

  • Networking opportunities

Application Deadline

There is no fixed deadline, as applications are accepted on a rolling basis depending on available internship roles.

Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible when roles are posted.

Read the frequently asked questions
Learn HERE for more information about the Mastercard Foundation Associates Program 2026.
To discover more global Internship opportunities, visit OFY HERE.

The post Mastercard Foundation Associates Program 2026: 12-Month Paid Internship Opportunity for Young African Graduates Across 22+ Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: GYBN Youth Fellowships 2026: Fully Funded Global Opportunities for Young Leaders in Biodiversity, Policy, Communication & Community Building
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The Global Youth Biodiversity Network (GYBN) Fellowship Program 2026 is now open, offering a transformative opportunity for young changemakers worldwide to engage in biodiversity conservation, youth advocacy, storytelling, policy engagement, and community development. Since its inception in 2022, the GYBN Fellowship Program has served as a powerful platform for youth-led action—supporting young leaders to design […]

The post GYBN Youth Fellowships 2026: Fully Funded Global Opportunities for Young Leaders in Biodiversity, Policy, Communication & Community Building appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Global Youth Biodiversity Network (GYBN) Fellowship Program 2026 is now open, offering a transformative opportunity for young changemakers worldwide to engage in biodiversity conservation, youth advocacy, storytelling, policy engagement, and community development.

Since its inception in 2022, the GYBN Fellowship Program has served as a powerful platform for youth-led action—supporting young leaders to design and implement impactful projects in their communities while connecting them to global environmental movements such as COP15 and COP16.

This year, multiple fellowship tracks are available, each designed to empower youth with skills, networks, and real-world experience to drive environmental and social change.

About the GYBN Fellowship Program

The GYBN Fellowship Program is a global initiative aimed at:

  • Supporting youth-led environmental action
  • Strengthening leadership capacity among young people
  • Promoting biodiversity conservation and sustainability
  • Amplifying youth voices in global policy spaces

Through its previous fellowship cycle (2024–2025), the program:

  • Supported 14 GYBN Chapters globally
  • Delivered 77+ community-based activities
  • Impacted 267+ communities, schools, and institutions

These initiatives included:

  • Landscape restoration
  • Environmental education programs
  • Youth mobilization campaigns
  • Indigenous and local community engagement
  • Creative activism and storytelling

Available GYBN Fellowships 2026

Applicants can apply to one of the following four fellowship tracks:

1. Youth Communications & Storytelling Fellowship

  • Focuses on amplifying youth voices and narratives
  • Supports creative storytelling, media, and communication strategies
  • Strengthens global youth engagement through impactful content creation

APPLY HERE

2. Youth Policy Support Fellowship

  • Engages fellows in biodiversity policy processes
  • Supports youth participation in regional and global advocacy spaces
  • Provides hands-on experience in policy coordination and systems change

APPLY HERE

3. Youth Community Building Fellowship

  • Focuses on strengthening grassroots youth networks
  • Encourages collaboration across cultures and regions
  • Builds solidarity and collective action within the GYBN network

APPLY HERE

4. Youth Capacity Building Fellowship

  • Develops learning spaces for youth leadership
  • Encourages peer-to-peer learning and knowledge sharing
  • Focuses on long-term leadership development in biodiversity conservation

APPLY HERE

Key Benefits of the GYBN Fellowship 2026

Participants in the program will gain:

  • Access to a global network of youth leaders
  • Opportunities to engage in international environmental platforms (e.g., COP events)
  • Hands-on experience in leadership, advocacy, and project implementation
  • Capacity-building through mentorship and collaborative learning
  • Exposure to global biodiversity and sustainability initiatives
  • Opportunity to lead impactful, community-based environmental projects

Eligibility Criteria

To apply for the GYBN Fellowship 2026, applicants must:

  • Be young individuals passionate about biodiversity and environmental action
  • Demonstrate leadership potential and commitment to community impact
  • Be actively engaged or interested in environmental, social, or youth-led initiatives
  • Be willing to collaborate in a global network
  • Have the ability to contribute to one of the fellowship tracks

Application Timeline

  • Application Deadline: 7 April 2026

Applicants are encouraged to apply early and ensure all required materials are submitted before the deadline.

Why You Should Apply

The GYBN Fellowship is more than just a program—it is a movement that empowers youth to:

  • Lead real environmental change in their communities
  • Gain international exposure and recognition
  • Build lifelong networks with global changemakers
  • Develop critical skills in leadership, policy, communication, and sustainability

This fellowship provides a unique opportunity to transform your passion for biodiversity into meaningful, measurable impact.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should:

  1. Select their preferred fellowship track
  2. Prepare all required application materials
  3. Submit their application through the official platform before the deadline

Click HERE to learn more about the GYBN Youth Fellowships 2026.

The post GYBN Youth Fellowships 2026: Fully Funded Global Opportunities for Young Leaders in Biodiversity, Policy, Communication & Community Building appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
